entries.definition:
ʿ-s-w to hope, to hope for, to expect, to anticipate, to look forward to, to be apprehensive, to fear. Of this root, only عَسَى ʿasā, occurs 30 times in the Qurʾan.
عَسَى ʿasā [verb of anticipation (فِعْلُ الرَّجَاءِ), always in the perfect, with following أَنْ and imperfect verb in the subjunctive] 1 hopefully (28:9) عَسَى أَن يَنفَعَنَا perhaps he may become of use to us 2 possibly (2:216) وَعَسَى أَن تَكْرَهُوا شَيْئًا وَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ it is possible that you may dislike something although it is good for you 3 it may come to pass, it may be ordained (66:5) عَسَى رَبُّهُ إِن طَلَّقَكُنَّ أَن يُبْدِلَهُ if he [the Prophet] decides to divorce you, it may come to pass that He will give him better wives in exchange for you.
entries.definition_english:

عَسَى ʿasā [verb of anticipation (فِعْلُ الرَّجَاءِ), always in the perfect, with following أَنْ and imperfect verb in the subjunctive] 1 hopefully (28:9) عَسَى أَن يَنفَعَنَا perhaps he may become of use to us 2 possibly (2:216) وَعَسَى أَن تَكْرَهُوا شَيْئًا وَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ it is possible that you may dislike something although it is good for you 3 it may come to pass, it may be ordained (66:5) عَسَى رَبُّهُ إِن طَلَّقَكُنَّ أَن يُبْدِلَهُ if he [the Prophet] decides to divorce you, it may come to pass that He will give him better wives in exchange for you.
